0.original: One day, I stopped in to see an old business friend. 2 00:00:03,600 --> 00:00:07,490 She had just returned from a conference with several of her executives. 3 00:00:07,680 --> 00:00:12,769 The moment I looked at her, I could tell there was something she wanted to get off her chest. 4 00:00:13,039 --> 00:00:16,529 She had the look of a woman who had suffered real disappointment. 5 00:00:16,880 --> 00:00:24,130 You know," she said, I called that conference this morning because I wanted some help on a proposed policy change. 6 00:00:24,320 --> 00:00:26,289 But what kind of help did I get? 7 00:00:26,480 --> 00:00:31,170 I had six men in there, and only one of them had anything to contribute. 8 00:00:31,440 --> 00:00:36,289 Two others talked, but what they said was just an echo of what I had said. 9 00:00:36,480 --> 00:00:39,250 It was like talking with a bunch of vegetables. 10 00:00:39,519 --> 00:00:43,410 I confess it's hard for me to find out what those fellows think. 11 00:00:43,840 --> 00:00:49,650 Really, she went on, you'd think those fellows would speak up and let me know what they think. 12 00:00:49,840 --> 00:00:52,850 After all, it directly affects each of them. 13 00:00:53,840 --> 00:00:56,610 My friend didn't get help in the conference. 14 00:00:56,720 --> 00:01:03,089 But had you roamed the hall after the meeting broke up, you'd have heard her junior associates making remarks like, 15 00:01:03,440 --> 00:01:08,370 I felt like saying, why didn't someone suggest, I don't think, 16 00:01:08,560 --> 00:01:10,210 we ought to go ahead. 17 00:01:10,640 --> 00:01:15,569 So, often the vegetables, those who have nothing to say in the conference room, 18 00:01:15,600 --> 00:01:20,850 are full of talk after the meeting, when what they've got to say won't make any difference. 19 00:01:20,880 --> 00:01:24,130 They're suddenly full of life when it's too late. 20 00:01:25,279 --> 00:01:27,890 Business executives want comment. 21 00:01:28,080 --> 00:01:31,890 The fellow who hides his light under a bushel hurts himself. 22 00:01:32,560 --> 00:01:34,450 Get the speak-up habit.
0.b1: One day, I visited an old business friend. She had just come back from a meeting with her managers. As soon as I saw her, I knew she had something bothering her. She looked very disappointed. 'You know,' she said, 'I held that meeting this morning because I needed help with a new policy. But what kind of help did I get? I had six men there, and only one had a good idea. Two others spoke, but they just repeated what I said. It was like talking to people who had no opinions at all. I admit, it is difficult for me to know what they are thinking. Really,' she continued, 'you would think they would speak up and share their thoughts. After all, the change affects them directly.' My friend did not get the help she needed in the meeting. However, if you had walked down the hall after the meeting, you would have heard her staff saying things like, 'I wanted to say, why didn't someone suggest that we shouldn't do it?' So, the people who said nothing in the meeting were full of talk afterward, when their words no longer mattered. They suddenly became active when it was too late. Business leaders want feedback. A person who hides their ideas hurts their own career. You should get into the habit of speaking up.
0.cn: 有一天，我去拜访一位老商业伙伴。她刚和几位高管开完会回来。我一看到她，就知道她有心事。她看起来非常失望。她说：“你知道吗，我今天早上召集那个会议是因为我想在拟议的政策变更上寻求帮助。但我得到了什么样的帮助呢？我在那里有六个人，只有一个人提出了有价值的建议。另外两个人也说了话，但他们只是重复我说过的话。就像是在和一群木头人说话一样。我承认，我很难知道那些人在想什么。真的，”她继续说道，“你本以为那些人会站出来告诉我他们的想法。毕竟，这直接影响到他们每个人。”我的朋友在会议上没有得到帮助。但是，如果你在会议结束后在走廊里转转，你会听到她的下属们说：“我当时想说，为什么没有人建议说，我不认为我们应该继续这样做。”所以，那些在会议室里什么都不说的人，在会议结束后却变得滔滔不绝，尽管他们说的话已经无济于事了。当一切太晚时，他们突然变得活跃起来。企业高管需要反馈。那个把自己的才华隐藏起来的人只会伤害自己。养成敢于发言的习惯吧。
0.ai_notes:
'stopped in': to visit someone briefly and informally.
'get off her chest': to talk about something that has been worrying you to feel better.
'executives': high-level managers who make important decisions in a company.
'proposed': suggested as a plan or idea.
'echo': to repeat someone else's opinion without adding new ideas.
'vegetables': a metaphor for people who are passive, quiet, or show no initiative.
'fellows': an informal way to refer to men or colleagues.
'speak up': to express your opinion clearly and honestly.
'broke up': when a meeting or event ends.
'junior associates': employees who are at a lower rank in the company.
'hides his light under a bushel': an idiom meaning to hide your talents or good ideas from others.
'speak-up habit': the practice of regularly sharing your thoughts and feedback.
0.remarks:
'stopped in': 短暂且非正式地拜访某人。
'get off her chest': 说出让你烦恼的事以减轻心理负担。
'executives': 公司里做重要决策的高层管理人员。
'proposed': 提议作为一项计划或想法。
'echo': 重复他人的观点而不提出新见解。
'vegetables': 比喻那些被动、安静或没有主动性的人。
'fellows': 对男性或同事的非正式称呼。
'speak up': 清晰且诚实地表达你的观点。
'broke up': 会议或活动结束。
'junior associates': 公司里级别较低的员工。
'hides his light under a bushel': 一个习语，意为隐藏自己的才华或好点子。
'speak-up habit': 经常分享想法和反馈的习惯。
